label: Process cosmology (Whitehead)

scope.school: Alfred North Whitehead's philosophy of organism, principally Process and Reality (1929)
limitations: Draft awaiting human domain review (D2-001); review needed before any scholarly authority is claimed.
Scoped to Whitehead's own system; Hartshorne's revisions and later process theology are out of scope.

No claims are recorded for origin, cosmic_structure, fundamental_problem, or epistemic_access as separate primitives: the sources used present the categoreal scheme rather than cosmography or soteriology. Absence means unspecified, not denial.

claims.claim: The world's final constituents are momentary experiential events: 'actuality is made up of atomic or momentary events. These events, called actual entities or actual occasions, are “the final real things of which the world is made up”' — 'each actual entity is a throb of experience'.
Creativity, not substance, is ultimate: 'creativity is the universal of universals—that which is only actual in virtue of its accidents or instances'.

God is dipolar: 'God, as well as being primordial, is also consequent … God is dipolar' — 'God's primordial nature is the permanent ground of value and determinacy and a storehouse for universals, or “envisaged potentialities”'.
Becoming is atomic rather than continuous: 'there is a becoming of continuity, but no continuity of becoming'.

Each event integrates its whole past — 'the many become one, and are increased with one' — and divine agency works by lure, not force: 'God's power over the world is described as persuasive rather than coercive. God cannot override the self-directed integrations'; 'God is the poet of the world, with tender patience leading it by his vision'.
Experience pervades nature: 'all individual actual entities—from electrons to human persons—are essentially self-determining and possess the ability to experience the world'.
claims.notes: How literally experience extends to all actual entities (panexperientialism) is a persistently debated element of process metaphysics in the secondary literature; the gloss here follows the IEP entry.

claims.claim: Living things are constitutively relational and value-laden: 'organisms are interdependent, internally and externally related, value-laden, active, and intrinsically active'.
The cosmic aim is enrichment of experience: 'God's purpose in the creative advance is the evocation of intensities', with God as 'the organ of novelty'.
What perishes is preserved in God's consequent nature: 'He saves the world as it passes into the immediacy of his own life'.

distinguishing_claims: Momentary experiential events, not enduring substances, are the final realities
Creativity is the ultimate category; even God is its foremost instance
God is dipolar and acts persuasively, never coercively
Perished occasions are everlastingly preserved in God's consequent nature
common_confusions: Process theism is not classical theism: God is neither omnipotent in the coercive sense nor immutable, which contradicts the classical-theist card.
Panexperientialism is not panpsychism about rocks and tables: aggregates differ from genuinely unified occasions of experience.
Whitehead's God is metaphysically required by the system, not an object of revealed religion.

sources.title: Alfred North Whitehead (Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy)
sources.passage: each actual entity is a throb of experience (1929c [1985: 190]). [...] there is a becoming of continuity, but no continuity of becoming (1929c [1985: 35]). [...] the many become one, and are increased with one (1929c [1985: 21]). [...] God is the poet of the world, with tender patience leading it by his vision (1929c [1985: 346]). [...] God, as well as being primordial, is also consequent … God is dipolar (1929c [1985: 345]). [...] God's purpose in the creative advance is the evocation of intensities (1929c [1985: 105]). [...] He saves the world as it passes into the immediacy of his own life (1929c [1985: 346]). (First published 1996-05-21; substantive revision 2022-11-10. Author byline not captured at access time — verify on review.)
